title: becium obovatum var. macrocaulon
intro: Becium obovatum var. macrocaulon, also known as East African wild basil, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to East Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its various health benefits. The health benefits of Becium obovatum var. macrocaulon include the potential treatment of wounds, fever reduction, and anti-inflammatory effects. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, to treat various ailments.
